message.content:
You are analyzing the PLM "tracking" service for a planned migration from GCP Cloud SQL to hosted Supabase Postgres.

Clone shallow; do NOT modify or commit:
  git clone --depth 1 --branch sandbox-albert https://github.com/popcre/designflow-tracking.git C:/repos/.dflow-audit/designflow-tracking
(If sandbox-albert fails, fall back to default `develop` and note it.)

~495KB. NOTE: shared-db docs say this branch (sandbox-albert) may include SAMPLE TRACKING tables/models (sample, sample_event, sample_comments, sample_attachment, sample_factory_group, sample_box, sample_shipment_item) that are ABSENT on main — confirm whether they're present here. Read ALL .md files, package.json, entrypoint, DB/connection config, Sequelize models (list them), routes, auth middleware, integrations.

Report structured markdown with EXACTLY these sections:
1. **Purpose & responsibility** — what "tracking" owns (sample tracking? design team time? production tracking?).
2. **Tech stack** — runtime, framework, ORM, key deps.
3. **Database access** — connection (GCP Cloud SQL? pool config — size/idle/keepalive — there's a known idle-connection-drop issue with Supabase pooler so note pool settings carefully), Sequelize models vs raw SQL, transactions, model count, shared models with other services.
4. **Auth** — request auth mechanism, middleware location.
5. **External integrations** — email, AI, file/storage, ERP, syncs, cron.
6. **API surface** — route shape and domains.
7. **Sample tracking** — confirm presence/absence of the sample* models/tables on this branch; list them.
8. **Heavy business logic** — logic that should stay in app code.
9. **Migration-relevant observations** — GCP coupling, env vars, model duplication with other PLM services, pool settings (note: there's an existing fix for Supabase pooler dropping idle connections requiring specific Sequelize pool settings).

Tight and factual, cite file paths.

1	# Designflow Tracking
2	
3	Tracking backend service for the Designflow PLM platform (production tracking, licensing
4	tracking, lead-time calculation, and **sample tracking**). Node/Express + Sequelize +
5	PostgreSQL. The service is private and sits behind the BFF; the browser reaches it at
6	`/api/tracking/*`.
7	
8	## Branch workflow
9	
10	Work only on `sandbox-albert`. Before adding code, sync GitHub `develop` into GitHub
11	`sandbox-albert`, pull GitHub `sandbox-albert` locally, then make changes. Push commits to
12	`sandbox-albert` and keep the PR from `sandbox-albert` to `develop` updated or create it if it
13	is missing. Never merge to `main`.
14	
15	Add or update unit tests for new behavior before committing.
16	
17	## Documentation map
18	
19	| Doc | Read it when |
20	|---|---|
21	| [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) | **Always first** — canonical guide: structure, custom-code boundary, data model, quirks, deployment, pending work |
22	| [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) | Claude Code-specific notes |
23	| [`docs/api-reference.md`](docs/api-reference.md) | You need the current tracking API route inventory |
24	| [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md) | System design, request flow, sample-tracking model |
25	| [`docs/development.md`](docs/development.md) | Local setup, run, test, debug |
26	| [`docs/configuration.md`](docs/configuration.md) | Environment variables and config files |
27	| [`docs/deployment.md`](docs/deployment.md) | Cloud Build → Cloud Run pipeline |
28	
29	## Stack
30	
31	- Node.js (CommonJS), Express
32	- PostgreSQL + Sequelize (models are the schema source of truth; no separate migration tool)
33	- JWT (RS256) auth via the BFF; DigitalOcean Spaces for sample photos
34	
35	## Quick start
36	
37	```bash
38	npm install
39	npm test          # Jest unit tests
40	npm run start     # NODE_ENV=localhost (runs npm test first, then nodemon)
41	```
42	
43	Requires `.env.localhost` with DB and JWT variables — see [`docs/configuration.md`](docs/configuration.md).
44	
45	## Health check
46	
47	`GET /api/health`
48	
49	## System interconnectivity
50	
51	This repo is the tracking service in the larger Designflow PLM system. It is
52	reached through BFF `/api/tracking/*` and owns production, licensing, lead-time,
53	and sample tracking while sharing item/order/user/factory data with the other
54	services through PostgreSQL. See
55	[`docs/system-interconnectivity.md`](docs/system-interconnectivity.md) for the
56	tracking-specific map and [`docs/system-architecture.md`](docs/system-architecture.md)
57	for the full multi-repo system map.

1	# AGENTS.md — designflow-tracking
2	
3	Canonical operating guide for developers and AI sessions working in this repo.
4	Read this first; it should give you a working mental model in under 5 minutes.
5	
6	## 1. Project summary
7	
8	`designflow-tracking` is a Node/Express + Sequelize + PostgreSQL backend service in the
9	Designflow PLM platform. It serves the **tracking** domain used by the Angular frontend
10	(`designflow-frontend`) via the BFF: production tracking, licensing tracking, lead-time
11	calculation, and — added in this work — **sample tracking** (physical product samples moving
12	between factories and the Ningbo/NYC offices). The service is private (Cloud Run
13	`--no-allow-unauthenticated`); all browser traffic reaches it through the BFF at
14	`/api/tracking/*`.
15	
16	## Multi-model AI note
17	
18	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
19	
20	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
21	
22	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
23

62	| `models/db.js` | Sequelize bootstrap + idempotent migration block | code we own |

131	## 6. Data model and external identifiers
132	
133	Sample tracking owns these tables (schema = `process.env.SCHEMA`, e.g. `designflow`):
134	
135	| Entity | Identifier / key | Where defined | Notes |
136	|---|---|---|---|
137	| Sample | `sample_id_pk` | `models/db/sample.js` | origin, direction, status, office_location, qty, retail_price, fob_cost; links `item_id_fk`→itemHeader, `prod_order_no_fk`→ProdOrderHeader, `customer_id_fk`→existing core `customers.customers_id`; courier tracking fields are `courier` + `tracking_number` |

139	| Sample comment | `id` | `models/db/sample_comments.js` | `sample_id_fk`, TEXT body, threaded via `parent_id` |

174	## 9. Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions
175	
176	### Samples are their own tables, not `itemHeader` rows
177	
178	Looks like: licensing tracking hangs off the shared `itemHeader`, so samples "should" too.
179	
180	Actually: samples are stored in dedicated `sample*` tables (production-tracking style).
181	
182	Why: `itemHeader` is owned by the item-master sync (`designflow-data-syncing` `findOrCreate`).
183	Injecting samples there would pollute the item master and risk them appearing as real items.
184	
185	Do not change because: it would couple samples to an externally-synced table and break the
186	item master. Cross-module links are done via `sample.item_id_fk` / `prod_order_no_fk` instead.
187	
188	### `db.js` populates the export synchronously, then awaits
189	
190	Looks like: `initialize()` is `async` but called without `await`, and `db` is mutated, not returned.
191	
192	Actually: model definitions are assigned to the exported `db` object **before** the first
193	`await` (Object.assign), then authenticate/sync/migrations are awaited.
194	
195	Why: `index.js` `startServer()` reads `db.LicenseFeedBacks.findAll()` synchronously right after
196	`require('./models/db')`. Awaiting before populating `db` leaves it empty at boot → crash.
197	
198	Do not change because: reordering an `await` above `Object.assign(db, …)` reintroduces the boot
199	crash. Guarded by `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.
200

1	var DBConfig = require('../config/db.config')()
2	var Sequelize = require('sequelize');
3	var db = {};
4	
5	initialize();
6	async function initialize() {
7	    // connect to db
8	    let sq_config = {
9	        dialect: 'postgres',
10	        host: DBConfig.HOST,
11	        schema: process.env.SCHEMA,
12	        logging: false,
13	        // Connection pool tuned for a managed/pooled Postgres (Supabase) reached
14	        // over TCP. The pooler silently closes client connections that sit idle,
15	        // so a long-lived idle Sequelize connection becomes a half-open dead
16	        // socket. Reusing it makes the next query stall on TCP retransmission for
17	        // 10-25s before the OS reports the socket is dead — which is exactly why
18	        // a rarely-hit endpoint like /sample/list intermittently blew past the
19	        // BFF's 30s proxy timeout and surfaced as "Failed to load samples".
20	        // Fix: don't hold idle connections (min:0), evict them quickly (well
21	        // before the pooler drops them), enable TCP keep-alive so any dead socket
22	        // is detected fast, and cap acquire/connect time below the BFF timeout so
23	        // a genuine connection problem fails fast instead of hanging for minutes.
24	        pool: {
25	            max: 22,
26	            min: 0,
27	            acquire: 20000,
28	            idle: 10000,
29	            evict: 5000,
30	        },
31	        createdAt: false,
32	        updatedAt: false,
33	        timestamp: false,
34	        dialectOptions: {
35	            connectTimeout: 15000,
36	            keepAlive: true,
37	            keepAliveInitialDelayMillis: 10000,
38	        }
39	    };
40	
41	    const sequelize = new Sequelize(DBConfig.DB, DBConfig.DBUser, DBConfig.PASSWORD, sq_config);
42	
43	    // Populate the exported `db` object SYNCHRONOUSLY (before any await). Model
44	    // definitions don't need an authenticated connection, and index.js calls
45	    // startServer() -> db.LicenseFeedBacks.findAll() synchronously right after
46	    // require('./models/db') returns. Mutate (Object.assign) rather than
47	    // reassign so the `module.exports = db` reference stays valid. Awaiting
48	    // before this line would leave consumers holding an empty object.
49	    Object.assign(db, require('./db/init-models')(sequelize))
50	    db.sequelize = sequelize
51	
52	    try {
53	        await sequelize.authenticate();
54	    } catch (error) {
55	        console.error('Unable to connect to the database:', error);
56	        throw 'Unable to connect to the database:', error
57	    }
58	
59	    // Models are the schema source of truth: sync() creates brand-new tables
60	    // (e.g. the sample-tracking tables) without altering existing ones.
61	    await sequelize.sync();
62	
63	    // Idempotent, awaited migration block for FUTURE column additions. Runs
64	    // AFTER sync() so it never races. Use snake_case identifiers only (mixed
65	    // case folds to lowercase in unquoted Postgres DDL and orphans the column).
66	    await applySampleMigrations(sequelize);
67	}
68	
69	/**
70	 * Additive, idempotent DDL for the sample-tracking module. Every statement is
71	 * IF NOT EXISTS and individually guarded so a re-deploy never crashes boot.
72	 */
73	async function applySampleMigrations(sequelize) {
74	    const schema = process.env.SCHEMA ? `"${process.env.SCHEMA}".` : '';
75	    const statements = [
76	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS sample_name VARCHAR(255)`,
77	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS quantity INTEGER`,
78	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS retail_price DECIMAL(12, 2)`,
79	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS fob_cost DECIMAL(12, 2)`,
80	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS customer_id_fk INTEGER`,
81	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS courier VARCHAR(20)`,
82	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS tracking_number VARCHAR(255)`,
83	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS factory_id_fk INTEGER`,
84	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS factory_group_id_fk INTEGER`,
85	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS box_id_fk INTEGER`,
86	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS office_location VARCHAR(20)`,
87	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS next_stop VARCHAR(50)`,
88	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS final_destination VARCHAR(50)`,
89	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS notes TEXT`,
90	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS item_id_fk INTEGER`,
91	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS prod_order_no_fk VARCHAR(255)`,
92	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "sample_createdTime" VARCHAR(255)`,
93	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "sample_createdUser" VARCHAR(255)`,
94	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "sample_modTime" VARCHAR(255)`,
95	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "sample_modUser" VARCHAR(255)`,
96	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample_box" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS final_destination VARCHAR(50)`,
97	        `ALTER TABLE ${schema}"sample_event" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS notes TEXT`
98	    ];
99	    for (const stmt of statements) {
100	        try {
101	            await sequelize.query(stmt);
102	        } catch (err) {
103	            console.warn('Sample migration skipped:', err?.message);
104	        }
105	    }
106	}
107	module.exports = db;

1	# Designflow Tracking API Reference
2	
3	This service handles production tracking, licensing tracking, lead-time
4	calculation, and sample tracking.
5	
6	In deployed environments callers should normally use the BFF:
7	
8	```text
9	https://api.designflow.app/api/tracking/<tracking-path>
10	```
11	
12	Direct local base:
13	
14	```text
15	http://localhost:5002
16	```
17	
18	## Auth
19	
20	Most routes require a Designflow JWT. The backend accepts the token in
21	`Authorization`. When called through the BFF, the BFF sends the user JWT as
22	`X-User-Authorization`, and the service remaps it to `Authorization`.
23	
24	Common roles:
25	
26	```text
27	admin, sourcing_manager, sales, designer, vendor, production
28	```
29	
30	## Route Mounts
31	
32	| Mount path | Router | Purpose |
33	|---|---|---|
34	| `/` | `lib.router.js` | Production tracking |
35	| `/lic` | `lic.router.js` | Licensing tracking |
36	| `/lead-time` | `lead-time.router.js` | Lead-time templates and calculation |
37	| `/sample` | `sample.router.js` | Sample tracking |
38	| `/api` | `health.router.js` | Health check |
39	
40	## Health
41	
42	| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
43	|---|---|---|---|
44	| `GET` | `/api/health` | none | Service health |
45	
46	## Production Tracking Routes
47	
48	Mounted at the service root.
49	
50	| Method | Path | Auth roles | Purpose |
51	|---|---|---|---|
52	| `GET` | `/getProdOrderHeader` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get production-order grid rows |
53	| `GET` | `/getViewlayout/:grid_id` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get production grid layout |
54	| `POST` | `/updateProdOrderHeader` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Update production-order header |
55	| `GET` | `/factoryProdOrders` | vendor | Get factory-visible production orders |
56	| `POST` | `/factoryProdOrderDates` | vendor | Update factory production dates |
57	| `POST` | `/syncPrepackAssociations` | production | Sync prepack associations |
58	
59	## Licensing Tracking Routes
60	
61	All paths below are mounted under `/lic`.
62	
63	| Method | Path | Auth roles | Purpose |
64	|---|---|---|---|
65	| `GET` | `/lic/getLic` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get licensing grid rows |
66	| `GET` | `/lic/getViewlayout/:grid_id` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get licensing grid layout |
67	| `POST` | `/lic/updateLic` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Update licensing row |
68	| `GET` | `/lic/statuses` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get license statuses |
69	| `GET` | `/lic/images/:itemId` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Get license image history |
70	| `POST` | `/lic/images` | designer, vendor, sales, production | Add license image history record |

1	# Architecture — designflow-tracking
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	A single Express service exposing the Designflow tracking domain. It is private (Cloud Run
6	`--no-allow-unauthenticated`) and is reached only through the BFF.
7	
8	```
9	Browser (Angular: designflow-frontend)
10	  → designflow-bff  (terminates user JWT, attaches Cloud Run OIDC token,
11	                     forwards user JWT as x-user-authorization)
12	  → designflow-tracking  (/api/tracking/* → service root paths)
13	  → PostgreSQL (Sequelize)        + DigitalOcean Spaces (sample photos)
14	```
15	
16	`index.js` first copies `x-user-authorization` back onto `authorization` so route guards
17	(`helpers/auth.js authRole`) see the user JWT even though the BFF replaced `authorization` with
18	the Cloud Run OIDC token.
19	
20	## Request layering
21	
22	```
23	routes/<x>.router.js     authRole([...]) gate, path → controller
24	controllers/<x>.controller.js   thin: unwrap req, call model, map errors to HTTP status
25	models/<x>.model.js      domain logic, callback style: (req, result) => result(err, data)
26	models/db.js → models/db/*.js   Sequelize models (schema source of truth)
27	```
28	
29	`routes/index.js` mounts: `/` (lib), `/lic` (licensing), `/lead-time`, `/sample`, `/api` (health).
30	
31	### Lead-Time Route Auth
32	
33	What changed:
34	`routes/lead-time.router.js` requires `authRole(...)` on every lead-time endpoint. Read and calculate routes allow logged-in users, while template update routes exclude `vendor`.
35	
36	Why:
37	Lead-time templates drive production date calculations and are global settings; vendor users should not be able to mutate them.
38	
39	Future sessions should:
40	Keep the read/write role split when changing lead-time endpoints and verify it with `tests/unit/lead-time.router.test.js`.
41	
42	## Persistence and migrations
43	
44	- `models/db.js` builds the Sequelize instance, populates the exported `db` object
45	  **synchronously** (model definitions don't need a live connection), then awaits
46	  `authenticate()` → `sync()` → `applySampleMigrations()`.
47	- `sequelize.sync()` creates brand-new tables from the models. Additive column changes go in
48	  the idempotent `applySampleMigrations()` block (`ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`,
49	  snake_case only). There is no separate migration CLI.
50	
51	## Sample tracking
52	
53	The sample module is modelled on production tracking (its own tables) rather than licensing
54	(shared `itemHeader`), because `itemHeader` is owned by the item-master sync.
55	
56	**Tables** (`models/db/`): `sample`, `sample_event`, `sample_comments`, `sample_attachment`,
57	`sample_factory_group`, `sample_box`, `sample_shipment_item`. Associations are wired in
58	`models/db/init-models.js`.
59	
60	**Lifecycle / state machine** (`helpers/sampleStatus.js`): a sample has an `origin`
61	(factory_request / factory_offer / usa_bought / customer) that sets its `direction`:
62	
63	- inbound: `requested → shipped → ningbo_checked_in → ningbo_checked_out → nyc_checked_in`
64	- outbound: `nyc_requested → nyc_checked_out → ningbo_checked_in → ningbo_checked_out → sent_to_factory`
65	- customer: `ningbo_checked_in → ningbo_checked_out → sent_to_customer`
66	- shared extras (any time): `cancelled`, `on_hold`, `damaged`, `approved`
67	
68	`recordEvent` derives the next status from `(direction, current status, office, event_type)`,
69	writes it under an optimistic guard (`WHERE status=<from_status>`, 0 rows → 409), and appends a
70	`sample_event` row. Boxes carry their own status independent of member samples; adding a
71	direction-mismatched sample to a box is skipped and reported (warn, not block).
72	
73	### Customer-Origin Samples
74	
75	What changed:
76	Customer-origin samples use `origin='customer'` and `direction='customer'`. They start at
77	`ningbo_checked_in`, then move through `ningbo_checked_out` to `sent_to_customer`. The normal
78	`POST /sample` endpoint accepts `origin: 'customer'`, and `POST /sample/customer` is a
79	convenience endpoint that forces that origin and defaults `office_location`, `next_stop`, and
80	`final_destination` to the Ningbo-to-customer path.
81	
82	Why:
83	These are physical samples sent directly from the Ningbo office to a customer, so they do not
84	fit the existing factory-to-NYC or NYC-to-factory lifecycles.
85	
86	Future sessions should:
87	Keep customer selection linked to the existing core `customers` table via `sample.customer_id_fk`
88	(`customers.customers_id`). Do not add a duplicate customer table to tracking.
89	
90	**Grouping**: `sample.factory_group_id_fk` (factory shipment group) and `sample.box_id_fk`
91	(current box cache) plus `sample_shipment_item` for per-leg box membership (a box can bundle
92	samples from multiple factory groups).
93	
94	**Integration links**: `sample.item_id_fk` (→ `itemHeader`) and `sample.prod_order_no_fk`
95	(→ `ProdOrderHeader`) let licensing/production attach samples to their records.
96	
97	**Customer / courier links**: `sample.customer_id_fk` points at the existing core
98	`customers.customers_id`. `sample.courier` is restricted to `ups`, `fedex`, `aae`, or `bombino`;
99	`sample.tracking_number` stores the carrier tracking number for the current shipment.
100	
101	**Photos**: presigned-URL upload to DigitalOcean Spaces (`helpers/digitalocean.js`):
102	`POST /sample/getPreSignedUrl` → browser PUT → `POST /sample/setObjectPublic` →
103	`POST /sample/photos` stores the public URL in `sample_attachment`.
104	
105	## Constraints
106	
107	- Service must stay private; the BFF is the only ingress.
108	- FKs are application-enforced (not DB constraints); the model layer owns cascade/integrity
109	  (e.g. `Sample.remove` deletes child events/comments/photos/shipment-items).
110	- `npm test` runs before boot in every `start:*` script.
111	
112	---
113	
114	## Cross-repo system map
115	
116	Tracking is reached through `designflow-bff` at `/api/tracking/*`. It owns
117	production, licensing, lead-time, and sample tracking while sharing item,
118	production order, user, vendor, factory, merch group, and grid metadata with the
119	other services through PostgreSQL. See
120	[`system-interconnectivity.md`](system-interconnectivity.md) for the
121	tracking-specific interconnectivity map and
122	[`system-architecture.md`](system-architecture.md) for the full
123	multi-repo architecture.
124

1	# Configuration — designflow-tracking
2	
3	Environment is loaded by `config/env.js` from `.env.<NODE_ENV>` (e.g. `.env.localhost`). In
4	deployed environments the values come from Cloud Build / Secret Manager (see
5	[`deployment.md`](deployment.md)), not from a committed `.env`.
6	
7	## Variables
8	
9	| Variable | Purpose | Source (prod) | Required dev | Required prod |
10	|---|---|---|---|---|
11	| `DB_HOST` | Postgres host | Secret Manager | yes | yes |
12	| `DB_PORT` | Postgres port | Secret Manager | yes | yes |
13	| `DB_USER` | Postgres user | Secret Manager | yes | yes |
14	| `DB_PASSWORD` | Postgres password | Secret Manager | yes | yes |
15	| `DB_NAME` | Postgres database | Secret Manager | yes | yes |
16	| `SCHEMA` | Postgres schema (e.g. `designflow`) | `--set-env-vars` | yes | yes |
17	| `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` | RS256 public key for verifying user JWTs | Secret Manager | yes | yes |
18	| `JWT_PRIVATE_KEY` | RS256 private key | Secret Manager | if signing locally | yes |
19	| `WEB_URL` | Frontend URL (startup log / context) | `--set-env-vars` | optional | yes |
20	| `API_ADDRESS` | API address (startup log / context) | `--set-env-vars` | optional | yes |
21	| `PORT` | Listen port (default `5002`) | Cloud Run `--port` | optional | yes |
22	| `DO_ACCESS_KEY` | DigitalOcean Spaces key | Secret Manager (`--set-secrets`) | for photo upload | yes |
23	| `DO_SECRET_KEY` | DigitalOcean Spaces secret | Secret Manager (`--set-secrets`) | for photo upload | yes |
24	| `DO_ENDPOINT_URL` | Spaces endpoint | `--set-env-vars` from `_DO_ENDPOINT_URL` | for photo upload | yes |
25	| `DO_BUCKET_NAME` | Spaces bucket | `--set-env-vars` from `_DO_BUCKET_NAME` | for photo upload | yes |
26	| `DO_PUBLIC_URL` | Spaces public CDN base | `--set-env-vars` from `_DO_PUBLIC_URL` | for photo upload | yes |
27	
28	`config/db.config.js` throws at startup if any of `DB_HOST/DB_PORT/DB_USER/DB_PASSWORD/DB_NAME`
29	is missing. The `DO_*` vars are read by `config/digitalocean.config.js` and only needed by the
30	sample photo-upload endpoints.
31	
32	> **Note (DigitalOcean):** `cloudbuild.yaml` provisions the DO keys via Secret Manager
33	> (`DO_ACCESS_KEY`, `DO_SECRET_KEY` — the `deployer@` runtime SA already has project-level
34	> `secretmanager.secretAccessor`) and the bucket/endpoint via `--set-env-vars`, with
35	> `_DO_ENDPOINT_URL`/`_DO_BUCKET_NAME`/`_DO_PUBLIC_URL` **defaulted in `cloudbuild.yaml`** to the
36	> shared `dflowbucket` Spaces values (same in sandbox + prod). No trigger change is required.
37	> Locally, put the `DO_*` values in `.env.localhost`.
38	
39	## Config files
40	
41	| File | Purpose |
42	|---|---|
43	| `config/env.js` | Selects/loads `.env.<NODE_ENV>`; exports `env`, `envFile` |
44	| `config/db.config.js` | Validates + returns DB connection settings |
45	| `config/digitalocean.config.js` | Maps `DO_*` env vars for the Spaces client |
46	| `config/columnOrderConfig.js` | Grid column ordering config |
47	
48	## Sequelize connection pool (`models/db.js`)
49	
50	The DB target is a **pooled/managed Postgres (Supabase) reached over TCP** — there is no Cloud SQL
51	unix socket. The pooler silently closes idle client connections, so the pool is tuned to avoid
52	stalling on a stale (half-open) socket. **Do not loosen these values** (see AGENTS.md §9 and the
53	SAMPLE-LIST-001 incident in §12):
54	
55	| Setting | Value | Why |
56	|---|---|---|
57	| `pool.min` | `0` | Never hold an idle connection that can go stale behind the pooler. |
58	| `pool.idle` | `10000` (10s) | Close idle connections before the pooler drops them. |
59	| `pool.evict` | `5000` (5s) | Run the idle-eviction sweep often enough for `idle` to take effect. |
60	| `pool.acquire` | `20000` (20s) | Fail fast — stays **below the BFF's 30s proxy timeout** instead of hanging. |
61	| `pool.max` | `22` | Unchanged; per-instance connection ceiling. |
62	| `dialectOptions.connectTimeout` | `15000` (15s) | Cap TCP connect under the 30s BFF timeout. |
63	| `dialectOptions.keepAlive` | `true` | OS TCP keep-alive so dead sockets are detected, not stalled on. |
64	| `dialectOptions.keepAliveInitialDelayMillis` | `10000` | Delay before the first keep-alive probe. |
65	
66	Symptom if reverted: the first request after an idle gap stalls 10–26s on a dead socket and the
67	30s BFF proxy returns 502 ("Failed to load samples" in the UI). Guarded by
68	`tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.

1	> ⚠️ **Auto-synced — do not hand-edit the copies.**
2	>
3	> [`u2giants/shared-db`](https://github.com/u2giants/shared-db) is the **single source of truth**. Its entire contents are automatically mirrored into the **`shared-db/` folder** of every consumer repo (CRM, DAM, PM, Directus, and the `popcre/designflow-*` repos) on each push to `main`, via [`.github/workflows/sync.yml`](https://github.com/u2giants/shared-db/blob/main/.github/workflows/sync.yml).
4	>
5	> **Reading this inside a consumer repo's `shared-db/` folder?** These files are a read-only copy — any edits here are **overwritten on the next sync**. Make changes in the canonical repo instead.
6	
7	---
8	
9	# Shared DB
10

1	# Deployment — designflow-tracking
2	
3	Derived from `Dockerfile` and `cloudbuild.yaml`.
4	
5	## Pipeline
6	
7	Cloud Build (`cloudbuild.yaml`) runs three steps:
8	
9	1. **Build** — `docker build -t ${_IMAGE} --build-arg NODE_ENV=${_APP_ENV} .`
10	   (`Dockerfile`: `node:lts-alpine`, installs `imagemagick`, `yarn` install, `CMD yarn
11	   start:$NODE_ENV` — which runs `npm test` then starts the server).
12	2. **Push** — pushes `${_IMAGE}` to Artifact Registry. Image path:
13	   `${_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${PROJECT_ID}/${_ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME}/${_APP_ENV}/${_APP_NAME}:${BUILD_ID}-${COMMIT_SHA}`.
14	3. **Deploy** — `gcloud run deploy ${_SERVICE_NAME}` with `--no-allow-unauthenticated`
15	   (private), `--vpc-connector`, `--service-account`, `--memory`, `--region`, `--port`,
16	   `--concurrency`, `--max-instances`.
17	
18	## Secrets and env at deploy
19	
20	- `--set-secrets`: `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME`, `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`,
21	  `JWT_PRIVATE_KEY`, `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY`, `DO_ACCESS_KEY`, `DO_SECRET_KEY` (all from Secret Manager
22	  `projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/...`; the `deployer@` runtime SA has project-level secretAccessor).
23	- `--set-env-vars`: `WEB_URL`, `API_ADDRESS`, `SCHEMA`, `DO_ENDPOINT_URL`, `DO_BUCKET_NAME`,
24	  `DO_PUBLIC_URL` (the `DO_*` ones come from `_DO_*` trigger substitutions).
25	- `_DO_ENDPOINT_URL`/`_DO_BUCKET_NAME`/`_DO_PUBLIC_URL` are **defaulted in `cloudbuild.yaml`**
26	  to the shared `dflowbucket` Spaces values (identical in sandbox + prod, verified against the
27	  core services), so no trigger change is needed. A trigger-level `_DO_*` substitution still
28	  overrides the defaults if a different bucket is ever required.
29	
30	## Trigger and substitutions
31	
32	All `_*` substitutions (`_REGION`, `_SERVICE_NAME`, `_APP_ENV`, `_APP_NAME`,
33	`_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME`, `_PORT`, `_CONCURRENCY`, `_MAX_INSTANCES`, `_VPC_CONNECTOR`,
34	`_PLATFORM`, `_MEMORY`, `_ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME`, `_WEB_URL`, `_API_ADDRESS`, `_DB_SCHEMA`) are
35	provided by the **Cloud Build trigger** (one per environment), not hard-coded in the repo. The
36	build runs when the branch the trigger watches receives a push.
37	
38	## Rollback
39	
40	Redeploy a previous image tag to the Cloud Run service:
41	
42	```
43	gcloud run deploy <service> --image <region>-docker.pkg.dev/<project>/<repo>/<env>/<app>:<previous-build-id>-<sha> --region <region>
44	```
45	
46	## Access / SSH
47	
48	The service is private; the **BFF** is the only ingress (it attaches the Cloud Run OIDC token).
49	There is no SSH-based deploy or admin path — deployment is image-based via Cloud Build/Cloud Run.
50	
51	## Pre-deploy gate
52	
53	`yarn start:$NODE_ENV` runs `npm test` first; a failing test suite stops the container from
54	starting. Keep the suite green before pushing the deploy branch.

# PLM Tracking Service: GCP Cloud SQL → Supabase Migration Analysis

## 1. Purpose & responsibility

**designflow-tracking** is a Node/Express + Sequelize backend service in the Designflow PLM platform, serving the **tracking domain** through the BFF at `/api/tracking/*`. It owns four distinct areas:

- **Production tracking** — production order visibility and date updates; vendor/factory-facing order date entry
- **Licensing tracking** — design licensing rows, status workflow, milestone images, and grouping
- **Lead-time calculation** — design team, licensing, and factory days templates with calculated needed-date backwards logic
- **Sample tracking** (primary focus) — physical sample item tracking: factory-to-Ningbo inbound, NYC-to-factory outbound, and Ningbo-to-customer direct shipments; includes events, photos, grouping, box/shipment management, and state machine

Sample tracking is new on `sandbox-albert` and represents the expansion of tracking beyond production/licensing into physical goods movement.

**File:** `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` (§1)

---

## 2. Tech stack

| Component | Details |
|---|---|
| **Runtime** | Node.js (CommonJS, not ES modules) |
| **Framework** | Express 4.22.1 |
| **ORM** | Sequelize 6.37.3 |
| **Database driver** | pg (node-postgres) 8.11.5 |
| **Database target** | PostgreSQL via `DB_HOST`/`DB_PORT` (currently GCP Cloud SQL, target is Supabase) |
| **Auth** | JWT RS256 via `helpers/auth.js`; BFF passes user JWT as `X-User-Authorization` header, remapped to `Authorization` in `index.js` |
| **File storage** | DigitalOcean Spaces (S3-compatible) via `@aws-sdk/client-s3` for sample photo uploads |
| **Testing** | Jest 29.7.0 (unit tests); Playwright 1.59.1 (e2e configured, not active) |
| **CI/CD** | Cloud Build (GCP) → Cloud Run (private, `--no-allow-unauthenticated`) |

**Files:** `package.json`, `index.js`, `config/`, `Dockerfile`, `cloudbuild.yaml`

---

## 3. Database access

### Connection & Pooling

The service connects to a **pooled/managed PostgreSQL** (currently GCP Cloud SQL, migrating to Supabase) over TCP (no Unix socket). Connection pool settings are **critical** because Supabase's pooler silently closes idle client connections; stale half-open sockets cause 10–26s TCP retransmission stalls and proxy timeouts.

**Current Sequelize pool config** (`models/db.js` lines 24–38):

```javascript
pool: {
    max: 22,
    min: 0,
    acquire: 20000,
    idle: 10000,
    evict: 5000,
},
dialectOptions: {
    connectTimeout: 15000,
    keepAlive: true,
    keepAliveInitialDelayMillis: 10000,
}
```

| Setting | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `pool.min` | `0` | Never hold idle connections (they go stale behind Supabase pooler) |
| `pool.idle` | `10000` (10s) | Evict connections before pooler drops them (typically 30s) |
| `pool.evict` | `5000` (5s) | Run idle-eviction sweep frequently |
| `pool.acquire` | `20000` (20s) | Fail fast; stays **below BFF 30s proxy timeout** |
| `pool.max` | `22` | Per-instance connection ceiling |
| `connectTimeout` | `15000` (15s) | TCP connect cap under BFF timeout |
| `keepAlive` | `true` | OS TCP keep-alive detects dead sockets immediately |
| `keepAliveInitialDelayMillis` | `10000` | Delay before first keep-alive probe |

**Known incident:** SAMPLE-LIST-001 — intermittent `/sample/list` 502 errors when idle intervals exceeded pooler drop time; fix verified in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.

**Connection requirements:**
- `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME` (validated by `config/db.config.js`)
- `SCHEMA` env var (e.g. `designflow`) — used in all queries

### Schema & Models

**No separate migration tool.** Sequelize models are the **schema source of truth**:
- `sequelize.sync()` creates new tables from model definitions
- Additive column changes go in idempotent `applySampleMigrations()` block (`ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`, snake_case only)

**35 Sequelize models** in `models/db/*.js`:

**Sample tracking** (7 tables, new on this branch):
- `sample` — primary sample record; links to `itemHeader` (item_id_fk) and `ProdOrderHeader` (prod_order_no_fk); customer shipments via `customer_id_fk` (existing core `customers` table)
- `sample_event` — append-only check-in/out/ship/receive/status_change events
- `sample_comments` — TEXT comments (threaded via `parent_id`); separate from shared `comments` table
- `sample_attachment` — photo metadata; `attachment_link` = DigitalOcean Spaces public URL
- `sample_factory_group` — grouping for factory shipments
- `sample_box` — Ningbo↔NYC shipment container; status independent of member samples
- `sample_shipment_item` — sample↔box membership per leg (factory_to_ningbo, ningbo_to_nyc, nyc_to_ningbo, ningbo_to_factory)

**Shared PLM core** (28 tables, owned by external services or synced):
- `itemHeader`, `itemDetail`, `itemAttachment`, `itemLicenseImage` — item master (synced by `designflow-data-syncing`)
- `ProdOrderHeader`, `ProdOrderDetail` — production orders (synced)
- `merchGroup`, `groups`, `users` — cross-domain references
- `LicenseFeedBacks`, `LicensingTime`, `DesignTeamTime`, `FactoryTime` — lead-time templates

**No explicit FK constraints.** Foreign keys are application-enforced; the model layer (`Sample.remove`, etc.) owns cascade/integrity.

**Transactions:** No explicit Sequelize transaction usage detected in models; operations are single-statement or rely on DB atomicity.

**Models population:** `models/db.js` populates the export **synchronously** before any `await` (critical quirk: `index.js` calls `db.LicenseFeedBacks.findAll()` synchronously right after `require('./models/db')`).

## 4. Auth

**Mechanism:** JWT RS256 via public key in `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` env var.

**Request flow:**
1. Browser → BFF with user JWT in `Authorization` header
2. BFF replaces `Authorization` with Cloud Run OIDC token, forwards user JWT as `X-User-Authorization` header
3. `index.js` middleware (lines 8–14) remaps `X-User-Authorization` back to `Authorization`
4. Route guards call `authRole(roles)` from `helpers/auth.js`

**Auth implementation** (`helpers/auth.js`):
- `authToken(req, res, next)` — verify JWT, decode into `req.user`
- `authRole(roles)` — gate route; check `decoded.role` against allowed roles (admin bypasses all checks)
- `getUserInfo(token)` — extract user from JWT

**Role inventory:**
- Core roles: `admin`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `designer`, `sales`, `vendor`
- Sample routes define access as: Read (admin, production, sourcing_manager, designer, sales, vendor), Create (admin, production, sourcing_manager, vendor), Write (admin, production, sourcing_manager)

**Files:** `helpers/auth.js`, `index.js` (lines 8–14), `routes/sample.router.js`

## 8. Heavy business logic

### Lead-Time Calculation

**Sequelize startup caches** (`index.js` lines 44–94):
- Loads all `LicenseFeedBacks`, `LicensingTime`, `DesignTeamTime`, `FactoryTime` into global memory
- Pre-builds mg_code → LicensingTime/FactoryTime maps by fuzzy-matching against mg_desc
- Avoids repeated fuzzy matching on every request

**Calculation flow:** `/lead-time/calculate` uses `LicensingTimeCache`, `MgLicTimesMap`, `MgFactoryTimesMap` to derive needed date backwards from delivery date.

**State machine risk:** Fuzzy matching logic in `helpers/leadTimeUtils.js` must be preserved across migration. Custom code, not data.

### Sample Status State Machine

**Core logic** (`helpers/sampleStatus.js`, 140 lines):
- Four origins (`factory_request`, `factory_offer`, `usa_bought`, `customer`) → two directions (`inbound`, `outbound`, `customer`)
- Per-direction ordered backbone statuses (5–7 states each)
- Five event types (`check_in`, `check_out`, `ship`, `receive`, `status_change`) trigger deterministic transitions
- Shared extra states (`cancelled`, `on_hold`, `damaged`, `approved`) reachable any time; `cancelled` is terminal
- Resume-from-hold back onto backbone

**Implementation** (`models/sample.model.js`, `helpers/sampleStatus.js`):
- `recordEvent()` calls `deriveToStatus()` to compute next status from (direction, current, office, event_type)
- Updates sample with optimistic guard (`WHERE status=<from_status>`, 0 rows → 409 conflict)
- Appends `sample_event` row (audit trail)

**Files:** `helpers/sampleStatus.js`, `models/sample.model.js` (recordEvent, ~150 lines)

### Grid Pagination & Filtering

AG Grid-compatible filter model on `/sample/list`:
- Server-side pagination: `startRow`, `endRow` → LIMIT/OFFSET
- Filter model: JSON; supports date, number, text filters (iLike case-insensitive, contains, equals, etc.)
- Whitelist guards (`FILTERABLE` set) prevent SQL injection via colId

Mirrors production/licensing grid filtering in `lib.model.js` and `lic.model.js`.

**Files:** `models/sample.model.js` (buildCondition, list, ~50 lines)

### Derived Fields

`GET /sample/list` returns two computed (non-stored) fields per row:
- `primary_photo` — the sample's primary `sample_attachment.attachment_link` (if exists)
- `box_label` — the `sample_box.box_label` for the sample's current `box_id_fk` (if exists)

Computed on read, not stored.

**Files:** `models/sample.model.js` (list method)

## 9. Migration-relevant observations

### GCP Cloud SQL Coupling

**Current setup:**
- `cloudbuild.yaml`: GCP Cloud Build (steps 1–3) → Artifact Registry → Cloud Run
- Secrets from GCP Secret Manager (`projects/$PROJECT_NUMBER/secrets/...`)
- Env vars via Cloud Build substitutions (`_REGION`, `_SERVICE_NAME`, etc.)
- No Cloud SQL unix socket; TCP connection over VPC connector

**Migration impact:**
- DB credentials (HOST, PORT, USER, PASSWORD, NAME) move from GCP Secret Manager to Supabase project
- Connection pool settings already tuned for **pooled/managed Postgres** (current config works for Supabase without change)
- VPC connector: Supabase has its own IP; may require firewall/network review
- **No GCP SDK calls in code** — no hard dependencies beyond Cloud Build deployment

**Files:** `cloudbuild.yaml`, `config/db.config.js`, `models/db.js`

### Model Duplication & Shared Ownership

**Shared tables across services:**
- `itemHeader`, `itemDetail`, `itemAttachment`, `itemLicenseImage` — owned by `designflow-data-syncing` (Coldlion sync); read by tracking
- `ProdOrderHeader`, `ProdOrderDetail` — synced; both tracking and other PLM services read
- `merchGroup`, `groups`, `users` — read-only references for tracking
- `comments` (shared) vs `sample_comments` (tracking-local) — intentional split (shared table caps at 50 chars; sample comments use TEXT)
- `externalCustomer`, `externalVendor` — read-only references

**Intentional isolation:**
- Sample tracking uses dedicated `sample*` tables, NOT `itemHeader` rows
- Reason: `itemHeader` is externally synced; injecting samples would pollute the item master and risk samples appearing as real items
- Links via `sample.item_id_fk`, `sample.prod_order_no_fk` instead (application-enforced)

**Risk:** Shared tables are synced by `designflow-data-syncing` and read by multiple services. Supabase migration must coordinate schema changes across all consumers.

**Files:** `AGENTS.md` (§9), `docs/architecture.md` (§8–9)

### Pool Settings & Supabase-Specific Tuning

**Critical:** Supabase pgBouncer pooler silently closes idle connections; stale half-open sockets cause intermittent 502 errors.

**Current settings already handle this:**
```javascript
pool: { min: 0, idle: 10000, evict: 5000, acquire: 20000, max: 22 }
dialectOptions: { connectTimeout: 15000, keepAlive: true, keepAliveInitialDelayMillis: 10000 }
```

**Do not loosen:**
- Keep `min: 0` (don't hold idle connections)
- Keep `idle: 10000` (evict before pooler drops them at ~30s)
- Keep `acquire: 20000` (fail fast, stays below BFF 30s timeout)
- Keep `keepAlive: true` (detect dead sockets fast)

**Known incident:** SAMPLE-LIST-001 — intermittent timeout on `/sample/list` when idle exceeded pooler drop time. Guarded by `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.

**Files:** `models/db.js` (lines 24–38), `docs/configuration.md` (§9), `AGENTS.md` (§9)

### Sequelize & Migration Approach

**Schema source of truth:** Sequelize models, not separate migrations.

- `sequelize.sync()` creates new tables from `models/db/*.js` definitions
- Additive columns: idempotent `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` in `applySampleMigrations()` function
- Snake_case only in DDL (unquoted identifiers fold to lowercase in Postgres)

**Supabase consideration:**
- Run `sync()` on first boot to create tables (safe; only creates if missing)
- Supabase migrations ledger is separate; don't commit to it unless coordinating with other apps
- `shared-db/` folder contains shared schema definitions for DAM, CRM, PM, and PLM

**Files:** `models/db.js`, `shared-db/README.md`, `shared-db/docs/`

### Shared Database Context

This repo includes a copy of `shared-db/` (auto-synced from `u2giants/shared-db`). The shared database unifies DAM, CRM, PM, and PLM on Supabase:

- **Canonical source:** `u2giants/shared-db` (not edited here)
- **Migration package:** `shared-db/supabase/migrations/` (foundation, core, domain_tables, RLS/realtime)
- **Preview branch:** `shared-db-schema-rehearsal` (persistent Supabase preview for testing)
- **Coordination:** All four apps (CRM, PM, DAM, PLM/tracking) share the same Supabase project

**Implication:** Tracking's schema changes must be coordinated through `shared-db` AGENTS.md contract. There are strict rules to prevent services from breaking each other.

**Files:** `shared-db/`, `shared-db/README.md`, `shared-db/AGENTS.md`

## Summary: Key Risks & Decisions

| Area | Current | Migration notes |
|---|---|---|
| **DB connection** | GCP Cloud SQL over TCP + VPC connector | Supabase Postgres + pooler; pool config already tuned; no changes needed |
| **Secrets** | GCP Secret Manager | Move to Supabase project secrets; `cloudbuild.yaml` substitutions stay the same |
| **Pool settings** | min=0, idle=10s, evict=5s, acquire=20s, keepAlive=true | **DO NOT LOOSEN**; already Supabase-optimized |
| **Model ownership** | sample* (tracking), shared item/order/user/merch/grid (synced/referenced) | Coordinate schema changes through `shared-db` AGENTS.md |
| **Lead-time caches** | Startup pre-builds mg_code maps from LicensingTime/FactoryTime | Stateless; no migration risk; caches refresh on boot |
| **Sample status SM** | Deterministic (direction, current status, office, event_type) → next status | Custom code; mirror in frontend `pages/sample_tracking/helpers/sample.status.ts`; must stay in sync |
| **DigitalOcean Spaces** | S3-compatible presigned URL + public ACL | No changes needed; same bucket in sandbox + prod (`dflowbucket`) |
| **Shared database** | Consolidating DAM, CRM, PM, PLM into one Supabase project | Track `shared-db/AGENTS.md` for cross-service coordination; four-app merge protocol |
